summary: Pass 10 of implicit-function-declaration cleanup moved large fighter type/enum/struct definitions out of `ft/fighter.h` into new `ft/types.h`, added missing headers/prototypes across player, fighter, stage, item, PAD, and game-mode code, and reorganized platform/MSL standard typedefs so `<stddef.h>` resolves through `src/MSL`. The PR also made matching-sensitive type fixes, notably introducing `uint` as `unsigned int` separate from `u32`/`unsigned long`, using `U32_MAX`/`F32_MAX`, and adjusting player APIs and KO counters to preserve unsigned-int comparison codegen.
what_changed: Added `src/MSL/stddef.h` with `wchar_t`, `ssize_t`, `size_t`, `intptr_t`, `uintptr_t`, and `NULL`; removed the old root `src/stddef.h` shim.
Added `src/MSL` to both `SYSTEM_INCLUDE_DIRS` in `Makefile` and `compile_flags.txt` so standard-style includes such as `<stddef.h>` resolve without using nonstandard `<MSL/stddef.h>` paths.

Updated `Runtime/platform.h` to include `<stddef.h>`, define `MWERKS_GNUC`, add `typedef unsigned int uint`, move stddef-like typedefs out, simplify `TRUE`/`FALSE`, remove `NULL`, and replace a local `FLT_MAX` macro with `U8_MAX`, `U16_MAX`, `U32_MAX`, and `F32_MAX`.

Moved roughly 2000 lines of fighter enums, action IDs, flags, structs, and fighter data declarations from `src/melee/ft/fighter.h` into new `src/melee/ft/types.h`; `fighter.h` now includes `ft/types.h`.
Replaced a large block of local `extern` declarations in `player.c` with real includes for fighter, game-mode, interface, player, and baselib headers.
Changed many `player.c`/`player.h` signatures and locals from `s32`/`u32` to matching-sensitive `int`, `uint`, or `enum_t`, and replaced magic sizes like `6`/`33` with `PL_SLOT_MAX`/`FTKIND_MAX`.
Changed `StaticPlayer::kos_by_player` from `s32[6]` to `uint[6]`, matching code that does unsigned comparisons while avoiding `u32` because that typedef maps to `unsigned long` and did not match.

Added or corrected prototypes in headers such as `Pad.h`, `ft_unknown_006.h`, `ftdata.h`, `code_801601C4.h`, `pl_unknown_001.h`, item/stage/interface headers, and related C files to eliminate implicit function declarations.

Touched `OSReset.c` to use more precise `BOOL` naming and control-flow style, add `MUST_MATCH` unused stack padding, call `__PADDisableRecalibration` through a declared prototype, and clean up reset constants.
Adjusted `math.h` pragmas to be guarded for Metrowerks and changed `lbcommand.c`/`ftlib.c` float-maximum initialization sites to use `F32_MAX`.
smart_moves: Moved shared fighter types to `ft/types.h` specifically to avoid circular dependencies instead of leaving large definitions in `fighter.h`.
Resolved standard-header layering by adding `src/MSL` to the include path, preserving standard-style `<stddef.h>` includes rather than hardcoding `<MSL/stddef.h>` in consumers.
Introduced `uint` to represent HAL-style/default `unsigned int` separately from project `u32` (`unsigned long`), which enabled matching for unsigned comparisons without lying about the ABI type.
Converted local implicit-declaration bandaids in `player.c` into real header dependencies, improving global type checking and reducing duplicated extern prototypes.
Used symbolic maxima (`U32_MAX`, `F32_MAX`) instead of magic literals where the decomp needed exact platform-sized constants while keeping callsites readable.
Used `MUST_MATCH` guarded unused stack arrays to preserve known stack layout/matching behavior without pretending the variables have semantic use.
decomp_lessons: When header inclusion creates cycles, split pure shared types/enums/structs into a dedicated `types.h` rather than forcing large subsystem headers to include each other.

`u32` is not always a safe replacement for an unsigned integer in matching work: in this tree `u32` is `unsigned long`, while some HAL code appears to rely on C `int`/`unsigned int` width and comparison behavior.
Fixing implicit-function-declaration warnings should usually mean adding/correcting prototypes in the owning header and including that header, not adding file-local externs.
Keep C standard library compatibility by putting MSL headers on the system include path; consumers should be able to write `<stddef.h>` and `<string.h>`-style includes.
Platform-wide typedef and constant knowledge belongs in `Runtime/platform.h`; standard typedefs and `NULL` belong in `stddef.h` according to review feedback.
For matching-sensitive unused locals, document them as unused stack and guard with `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` so intent is clear.
Replacing magic array bounds with known enum/slot constants like `FTKIND_MAX` and `PL_SLOT_MAX` improves safety while staying faithful when the constants are already established.
naming_conventions: `src/melee/ft/types.h` uses include guard `MELEE_FT_TYPES_H` and centralizes fighter type names such as `FighterKind`, `CharacterKind`, `ftCommonAction`, `Fighter`, and fighter flags.
Use `PL_SLOT_MAX` instead of literal player slot count `6` and `FTKIND_MAX` instead of literal fighter mapping count `33`.
Use `uint` for HAL/default unsigned-int cases where `u32`/`unsigned long` breaks matching.
Use all-caps max-value macros in platform headers: `U8_MAX`, `U16_MAX`, `U32_MAX`, `F32_MAX`.
Boolean macros were normalized from parenthesized `#define TRUE (1)` / `FALSE (0)` to `#define TRUE 1` / `FALSE 0`.
Helper naming was cleaned up from `hasExtraFighterID` to `hasExtraFighterId`, matching project-style mixed-case `Id` rather than all-caps `ID` in that local helper.
Unknown or matching-only data was documented with `@todo`, including comments such as unused stack and suspected duplicate structs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed `kos_by_player` and related accessors to `uint` so comparisons such as `kos < U32_MAX` match unsigned-int behavior; review explicitly notes `u32` would not match.
Kept exact float maximum semantics through `F32_MAX 3.4028235E38`, used in `ftlib.c` min-distance/min-diff initialization and discussed for `lbcommand.c` timer initialization.
Added `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` unused stack byte arrays in player and OS reset code to preserve stack layout only when matching is required.
Changed function parameter and local types in player routines from fixed-width aliases to `int`, `uint`, or `enum_t` where evidence indicated generated code depended on default C integer types.
OS reset cleanup preserved control-flow shape with explicit `continue` in loops and `while (...) continue;` patterns in places likely relevant to matching.
Moved standard typedefs out of `Runtime/platform.h` into MSL `stddef.h`, then included `<stddef.h>` from platform to keep type availability without duplicate typedef definitions.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Fighter definitions were moved to `ft/types.h` to avoid circular dependencies.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: `u32` would not match for `kos_by_player`; the field is used in unsigned comparisons, supporting the idea that HAL's default data type was `int` rather than `long`.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Including a standard header through a nonstandard path defeats the purpose; `src/MSL` should be on the include path.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested using standard `FLT_MAX`; subsequent discussion accepted leaving a hardcoded/project `F32_MAX` for now to guarantee f32 bit-for-bit behavior.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: `NULL` also belongs in `stddef.h`, though the reviewer said it did not need to be fixed there immediately.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further split large subsystem headers into `types.h` files to break circular includes.
Search for remaining uses of `u32` where `uint` or `int` may be required for unsigned-int matching.
Audit whether `NULL` fully migrated to MSL `stddef.h` and whether all consumers include it through `<stddef.h>`.
Look for later resolution of the `F32_MAX` versus `FLT_MAX` platform constant discussion.
Search for `MUST_MATCH` unused stack comments added after this PR to identify reusable stack-preservation patterns.
